Name: Fire Bomb
Flavor: Light them up.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to make a ranged special attack using your DEX against a target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, you deal **2d6 fire damage** to the target. Alternatively, you may instead make a Wreck an Object check against the target and add \+2 to the total.

Regardless if the check succeeds or fails, you also designate a 10 foot radius sphere originating from the target. The target and all creatures and objects in the area are dealt an additional **1d8 fire damage**.

Name: Stormcaller Dart
Flavor: Electric filaments trail the trajectory as bolts of lightning hone in on the target.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to make a ranged special attack using your DEX against a target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, you deal **4d6 lightning damage** to the target. Alternatively, you may instead make a Wreck an Object check against the target and add \+4 to the total.

Regardless if the check succeeds or fails, you also designate a 10 foot radius, 60 foot tall cylinder originating from the target. The target and all creatures and objects in the area are dealt an additional **2d8 lightning damage**. Affected creatures lose a reaction if they had one.

Name: Frost Snare
Flavor: Shimmering icicles weave into frozen netting as they burst out of the bomb.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to make a ranged special attack using your DEX against a target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, you deal **6d6 cold damage** to the target. Alternatively, you may instead make a Wreck an Object check against the target and add \+6 to the total.

Regardless if the check succeeds or fails, you also designate a 10 foot radius sphere originating from the target. The target and all creatures and objects in the area are dealt an additional **3d8 cold damage** and become stuck with the **Recoverable** condition trait, allowing an action to end the effect.

Name: Caustic Flask
Flavor: With how much it steams and bubbles inside, there is no question about how it is meant to be used.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to make a ranged special attack using your DEX against a target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, you deal **8d6 acid damage** to the target. Alternatively, you may instead make a Wreck an Object check against the target and add \+8 to the total.

Regardless if the check succeeds or fails, you also designate a 10 foot radius sphere originating from the target. The target and all creatures and objects in the area are dealt an additional **4d8 acid damage**. Affected creatures cease to have any effect that grants them their Half Guard AC or their Full Guard AC.

Name: Acidic Smear
Flavor: Dripping acid sizzles as it splatters on the ground while you prepare a deadly offensive.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **acid damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Condensed Gloom
Flavor: A thick, droopy gloom coating your weapon, drawing in life and moisture with each passing second.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **necrotic damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Electrostatic Oil
Flavor: You lose the sense of weight from the weapon while energetic sparks fly erratically.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **lightning damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Flame Grease
Flavor: A translucent, boiling goo that ignites as it leaves the ceramic capsule.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **fire damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Frostbitten Sheen
Flavor: Iridescent ice crystals form along the edge of the weapon as you apply the finishing touches.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **cold damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Parallel Polish
Flavor: Hueshifted replicas of the weapon follow in its wake as many worlds are drawn to a single point.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **force damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Sunspark Glitter
Flavor: A mass of twinkling lights blends together to form intricate, glowing patterns superimposed over your weapon.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon you are holding for the next 10 minutes. A weapon may not be coated by more than one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. Weapon attack hits made with a weapon coated by this item gain the following benefits:

* All of the damage dealt is converted to **radiant damage**.

* The attack's damage roll is increased by **1d6/2d6/3d6/4d6 damage**.

* Damage taken reduction from DBs is halved against damage dealt by the attack.

Name: Flagon of Focus
Flavor: The vapor burns the nostrils, and the heat of the taste is echoed by the coal in the mind's fire.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 10 minutes:

* **Brilliant.** Your INT becomes \+3/+4/+5/+6.

* **Atrophy.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your STR is reduced by 2\.

Name: Mixture of Muscle
Flavor: A powdery residue clings to the sides of the container, and it smells of meat.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 10 minutes:

* **Muscular.** Your STR becomes \+3/+4/+5/+6.

* **Ketosis.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your INT is reduced by 2\.

Name: Potion of Posing
Flavor: Cool smoke emanates off the blue surface of the liquid steel and from your mouth on the exhale.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 10 minutes:

* **Impressive.** Your CHA becomes \+3/+4/+5/+6.

* **Inhibition Loss.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your CON is reduced by 2\.

Name: Solution of Satiation
Flavor: A rich, caramelized syrup is nutritious and delicious. A whole vial full of drops that could individually satisfy hunger for a day.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 10 minutes:

* **Hardy.** Your CON becomes \+3/+4/+5/+6. Potion toxicity is still calculated with your unmodified CON.

* **Flatulence.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your CHA is reduced by 2\.

Name: Stein of Sense
Flavor: Flavorless, colorless, but practical in its uses. Don't mistake it for water.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 10 minutes:

* **Savvy.** Your WIS becomes \+3/+4/+5/+6.

* **Overanalysis.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your DEX is reduced by 2\.

Name: Tumbler of Tumbling
Flavor: Buzzing with bubbles, this fizzy concoction propels its user faster than they themselves can see.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 10 minutes:

* **Lithe.** Your DEX becomes \+3/+4/+5/+6.

* **Impulsivity.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your WIS is reduced by 2\.

Name: Acceleration Tonic
Description:
*A very dense, gray, metallic liquid pulls away from the glass and forms a dome at the top of the bottle.*

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Racing Heartbeat.** You add 10 feet to your Speed.

* **Bradycardia.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you must move at least 10 feet each turn or become stunned until the beginning of your next turn.

Name: Flippergulp
Flavor: This immiscible solution separates into air on the bottom and liquid on top. Shaking before drinking ensures you only get the best parts of being a fish.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next long rest:

* **Amphibious.** You are able to breathe underwater.

* **Webbed Toes.** You gain advanced Swimming mobility.

* **Piscine Features.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you gain visible scales and cannot blink.

Name: Philter of Awareness
Flavor: The deep blue liquid is refreshing both on the way down and if poured on oneself after victories.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects for 8 hours:

* **Reflexes.** You gain your Half Guard AC.

* **Nerve Pinch.** While suffering from potion toxicity, taking damage causes you to become stuck until the end of your next turn.

Name: Razor Dose
Flavor: A thin, crystalline layer sits on top of this colorless liquid that snaps when shaken.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Focus.** Your weapon attacks' critical hit threshold is reduced by 1\.

* **Hyperfocus.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your enemies' weapon attacks against you also have their critical hit threshold reduced by 1\.

Name: Shrink Juice
Flavor: The scent reminds one of mushrooms and insanity, but the blue bubbles are enticing.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Controlled Atrophy.** You become one Size smaller.

* **Fatigue.** While suffering from potion toxicity, your STR is reduced by 1\.

Name: Attercup
Flavor: The mesh of wiry filaments in the solution is hard to swallow, but it dissolves on the way down.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Scopulae.** You gain advanced Climbing mobility.

* **Skittish.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you incur disadvantage on **Courage** saving throws.

Name: Colossal Cordial
Flavor: An extraordinarily dense solution with red flecks that foams when drunk.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Growth.** You become one Size larger.

* **Growing Pains.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you take **1 poison damage** at the end of each turn if you traveled at least 30 feet during that turn.

Name: Limb Libation
Flavor: The charred eye of a young kraken sizzles at the bottom of the flask, trapped in its final moments of agony.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Elongation.** Your Reach is increased by 5 feet.

* **Constriction.** Whenever you succeed on a Grapple or Pin check, you deal **2d4 bludgeoning damage** to the target of the action.

* **Flammable.** While suffering from potion toxicity, **fire damage** dealt to you is doubled and sets you ablaze.

Name: Slime Salve
Flavor: An almost chalky texture, but with a mossy flavor. This hazardous sludge invites only the daring.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Toxic Skin.** When you are hit with a melee attack, you deal **5 acid damage** or **5 poison damage** to the attacker.

* **Autoimmune.** You gain DB 5 against **acid** and **poison damage**.

* **Slippery.** While suffering from potion toxicity, using prone's **Recoverable** trait requires an additional action.

Name: Perspicacity Drop
Flavor: This translucent, colorless liquid rotates more slowly than the bottle when jostled. When poured, its faceted inner structure gives way to a crisp finish.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Clarity.** You gain advantage on **Quick-Wits** and **Will** saving throws.

* **Hyper-autoawareness.** While suffering from potion toxicity, ST regained from burning a Resolve is halved.

Name: Bladeward Brew
Flavor: An azure solution that settles into a splintery precipitate. Shake before drinking.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Stone Skin.** You may act as if you are wearing Stock Armor and without needing to meet its STR requirement

* **Calcified.** You gain DB 15 against **physical damage**.

* **Gout.** While suffering from potion toxicity, when your DB from this potion breaks, you take **5 injuring piercing damage**.

Name: Lifting Drink
Flavor: Drunk upside-down, the rising liquid fills your sinuses with a cooling burn.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Upper.** You gain basic Hovering mobility.

* **Downer.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you incur disadvantage on STR and DEX checks.

Name: Lightning Stim
Flavor: Electric anticipation bubbles within this concentrated shot of energy.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Overclock.** All attack checks you make gain advantage.

* **Overdrive.** Whenever you hit a creature or object with an attack twice within a single turn, you add **6d6 lightning damage** to the damage roll of the second attack.

* **Meltdown.** While suffering from potion toxicity, half the damage you deal with **Overdrive** is also dealt to you.

Name: Photonic Flask
Flavor: Vaporized sunlight scatters out of the opaque bottle as it is uncorked, disappearing in a flash if not immediately consumed.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Lightweight.** Your Speed is doubled.

* **Cardio.** At the end of each of your turns, you **regain ST** equal to half the amount of feet you traveled in that turn.

* **Endorphin Glow.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you cannot benefit from being invisible.

Name: Rotgut
Flavor: Putrid ooze permeates through the glass, grows on its surface, and emits a sweet stench.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Bloating.** As a free action once per turn, when you are dealt damage, you may deal **12 necrotic damage** to all creatures and objects within 10 feet of you.

* **Engorged.** You and other creatures within 10 feet of you are slowed and the damage they deal to you is halved.

* **Flatulence.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you have disadvantage on CHA checks.

Name: Spirits of Severance
Flavor: The weight within the bottle sporadically shifts as parts of the solution phase in and out of the physical realm.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Dematerialization.** Attack checks against you incur disadvantage.

* **Phase Shift.** At the end of your turn, you may choose to become *phased out* until the start of your next turn. While phased out, you are unable to affect other subjects and cannot be affected by subjects other than yourself.

* **Dissociation.** While suffering from potion toxicity, instead of choosing whether to use **Phase Shift** at the end of your turn, you make a DC 11 flat check and phase out on a failure.

Name: Divinity Dram
Flavor: Made from rare, celestial flowers, this mother-of-pearl solution contains gold flakes that drift in sacred spirals.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Heavenly.** You gain a \+5 bonus to all attack and combat checks.

* **Euphoria.** While suffering from potion toxicity, when you take damage, you become pacified until the end of your next turn.

Name: Misfortune Inoculation
Flavor: The sour taste of disappointment lingers on your palate, preparing your body to defend against the worst of fates.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Critical Immunity.** Critical hits scored against you instead count as normal hits.

* **Metal Macrophages.** When an attack made against you misses, you gain your Full Guard AC until the start of your next turn.

* **Adverse Equilibrium.** While suffering from potion toxicity, critical hits you score instead count as a normal hit.

Name: Transparency Draft
Flavor: A fluid so clear that the bottom of the flask vanishes before your eyes.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to gain the following potion effects until the end of your next rest:

* **Translucent.** You are invisible.

* **Photofriction.** While suffering from potion toxicity, you are slowed.

Name: Poultice
Flavor: Though it takes time to set, a quick solution can often work just as well.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	You may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach either as a Use Item action or over the course of one minute. Depending on the time taken, this item grants different benefits to the target:

* **As a Use Item action,** the target **regains 20/40/60/80 ST**.

* **Over one minute,** the target **heals 5/10/15/20 HP**.

Name: Antidote
Flavor: A fermented shot of wellness for the ailing.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, cleansing the target of the disoriented and poisoned conditions.

Name: Nightmare Solvent
Flavor: Sloshing within the bizarre putty are boundless imaginations. All your fears and confusions just melt away.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, cleansing the target of the disoriented and frightened conditions.

Name: Slip Grease
Flavor: If only the lid would stay on.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, removing the grappled, stuck, and restrained conditions from the target.

Name: Ecliptic Elixir
Flavor: Twinkling starlets bespeckle the solution as floating dust. A gaping void is suspended in the liquid, obscuring the wondrous display behind it.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, cleansing the target of the poisoned and polymorphed conditions.

Name: Liquid Courage
Flavor: It's probably just whiskey, but half the effect comes from believing in it.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, cleansing the target of the frightened and incapacitated conditions.

Name: Distilled Clarity
Flavor: An aftertaste that separates out the individual flavors of what came before. The pieces of the puzzle have been slotted into place.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, cleansing the target of the disoriented and stunned conditions.

Name: Panacea
Flavor: A kaleidoscopic concoction that shifts in color and texture when in proximity of the patient. It truly cures all.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may expend this item by administering it to a target creature within your Reach, cleansing the target of all negative conditions.

Name: Deadly Poison
Flavor: It's not about sending a message - it's about ensuring there is no receiver.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

	When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is dealt **2d8/4d8/6d8/8d8 poison damage**.

Name: Deadly Serum
Flavor: A variant extract concocted for directed mental attacks.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

	When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is dealt **2d8/4d8/6d8/8d8 psychic damage**.

Name: Sickening Poison
Flavor: Catching even a whiff of the foul stench can make you feel queasy.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

	When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is poisoned. At the end of an affected creature's turn, they must make a DC 14 **Fortitude** saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Twisted Serum
Flavor: A frothy fluid that never seems to come to rest. Staring into it for too long can induce a minor loss of balance.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is disoriented. At the end of an affected creature's turn, they must make a DC 14 **Quick-Wits** saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Repulsive Serum
Flavor: A refined distillation of pure terror.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is frightened of the attacker. At the end of an affected creature's turn, they must make a DC 17 **Courage** saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Tranquil Poison
Flavor: A glossy, translucent solution favored for silent takedowns.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

	When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target falls unconscious if they have no Resolve remaining.

Name: Dazing Poison
Flavor: The scarlet hue and complete sense of numbness are both signatures of this toxin.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

	When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is incapacitated. At the end of an affected creature's turn, they must make a DC 20 **Fortitude** saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Newtative Serum
Flavor: They might get better.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.
* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

	When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target must make a DC 20 **Fortitude** saving throw. On a failure, the target is polymorphed into a newt, a Tier 0 beast creature. At the end of an affected creature's turn, they may retry the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Horror Serum
Flavor: Appearing different to each observer, the heinous slime reflects your own fears with cruel precision.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is affected by the following:

* **Fear.** The target is frightened of every creature.

* **Hopelessness.** This frightened condition has the **No Spellcasting** condition trait, preventing the affected creature from casting spells.

* **Panic.** The target, if they were concentrating on a spell effect, loses concentration.

* **Psychosomatic Pain.** While frightened, melee attacks that hit the creature automatically score a critical hit. All damage dealt by these attacks is converted to **psychic damage**.

At the end of an affected creature's turn, they must make a DC 23 **Courage** saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Stunning Poison
Flavor: A potent venom employed by dangerous monsters to stun their prey.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to *coat* a weapon or three pieces of ammunition you are holding until the end of your next long rest. An item may only be coated by one oil or toxin consumable item at a time. The type of item you coat determines when the toxin's effect is applied:

* **Ammunition.** When you make a ranged weapon attack with the coated ammunition, the ammunition is expended and the below effect is applied if the attack hits.

* **Weapon.** On the first time you hit with a melee weapon attack using the coated weapon, the below effect is applied.

When this toxin is applied to a creature, the target is stunned. At the end of an affected creature's turn, they must make a DC 23 **Fortitude** saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

# **List of Artisan Items**

Name: Bountiful Vessel
Flavor: This vessel can be found in many aesthetic designs, but no self-respecting chef in a magical world is without one.
Description: As a Use Item action, you dispense a specific liquid from the list below at a maximum rate of 2 gallons per minute: any alcoholic beverage, coffee, tea, mayonnaise, oil, fresh or salt water, or vinegar.

Name: Dovelight
Flavor: The soul of a lucent bird caged within a clockwork body, its trills and coos replicated with whirs and clicks.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may cast **Spectral Lantern** using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Obsidian Glove
Flavor: A hand of volcanic glass that erupts to life at the snap of your fingers.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may cast **Glass Hand** using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Planter Pouch
Flavor: A small burlap sack filled to the brim with seeds ready for planting.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may cast **Sentry Rose** using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Prismatic Brush
Flavor: Flecks of paint adorning the tips of the brush shimmer with vibrant hues.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may cast **Repaint** using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Reformer's Mallet
Flavor: Just keep hitting it until it works.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may cast **Mending** using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Wayward Compass
Flavor: The tumbling needle within points not in a direction but in the sense of a direction.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may cast **Touchstone** using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Curious Contraption
Flavor: The shutter flickers to allow a brief instant to be captured by the luminous eye encased within.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Shard of Memory** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Paradoxical Pouch
Flavor: It's just false bottoms all the way down.
Description:
This Tiny container holds within it an extra-dimensional space that is up to 64 cubic feet in volume. When you stow an object, you may instead put it in the container, but you must take a Use Item action to retrieve an item from the container. All objects stowed in the container spill out if the container is destroyed.

	A creature occupying the container begins asphyxiating (as described by the Swimming mobility) after 6 hours spent inside.

Name: Potion Gun
Flavor: The exact solution to your problem, delivered by the fastest and most cathartic method.
Description: As an object interaction, you may *load* a potion or remedy stowed on you into this item. Use a Use Item action, you may expend the loaded item to apply its effects to a target creature within 30 feet of you.

Name: Prying Phial
Flavor: Wisps of twinkling smoke waft out of the stopper, drifting towards an adversary of your naming.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Tracker Sense** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Psionic Receiver
Flavor: A flask of bubbling liquid, mediating and morphing thoughts between those attuned to it.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Telepathic Bond** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Spectral Chain
Flavor: A vestige of life force awakens an iron grip upon this chainlink wire.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Pearlescent Cable** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Spelly Putty
Flavor: Squeeze the putty. Be the putty.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Mimic Matter** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Asynchronic Stopwatch
Flavor: The whir of the timepiece's machinery is your only motion in the ever-moving universe, as you watch it drift by for a moment, only to snap back into reality a few feet away.
Description:
As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Riftwalk** at 2nd Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier with the following heightening enhancement automatically chosen:

* You may bring a target willing creature within your Reach with you, teleporting them with you.

Name: Dome-icile
Flavor: This tiny wooden bowl, carved with runes, can expand to form a dome of force capable of providing a comfortable and safe resting area for you and your companions.
Description:
Once per rest, you may cast **Warding Circle** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

When you use this item to cast **Warding Circle**, the designated area is instead a 15 foot radius sphere, it does not have its **Permanence** trait, and you may designate all creature type tags then choose any number of creatures to be unaffected by the spell effect.

Name: Forgotten Effigy
Flavor: An enigmatic figurine whose ghostly image appears to beckon you forward, forever cursed to lead its handlers down paths untrodden and out of sight.
Description:
As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Cloak** at 2nd Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier with the following heightening enhancement automatically chosen:

* You may choose for the casting of this spell to be imperceptible.

Name: Living Chainmail
Flavor: A semi-sentient web of metal threads crackling and roiling with nervous energy, capable of fending off attackers with electric flicks if left to its own devices or reinforcing a structure if properly coaxed.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast either **Defender Drone** or **Fortify** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Mirror of Aspiration
Flavor: A window of pale silver that casts its onlookers with the trappings of their ever-shifting desires.
Description:
As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Disguise** at 2nd Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier with the following heightening enhancement automatically chosen:

* An affected creature may take an action to change their appearance again to a new one of their choosing.

Name: Verdant Switch
Flavor: A wooden staff that never lost its spark of life, fostering vibrant microcosms within the gnarls and knots of the ancient wood.
Description:
As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Climbing Vine** at 2nd Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier with the following heightening enhancement automatically chosen:

* The climbing vine's Toughness becomes 15\.

Name: Apogeic Tether
Flavor: A divergent hook catching the convergent threads of the universe, allowing you to zip towards its vertex at nauseating speeds.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Leyline Jaunt** at 4th Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Concordant Coin
Flavor: A small token of understanding with identical imagery imprinted on both sides.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Thoughtstream** at 4th Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Mighty Whirlybird
Flavor: A stained-glass hummingbird that pulls your ride aloft with astonishingly little effort.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Night Ride** at 4th Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Planar Key
Flavor: A key without a keyhole without a door without a wall.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Transplanar Crossroads** at 4th Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Tabula Vista
Flavor: A slate of dynamic earth molding itself into the topography and fauna of the land around you.
Description: As a Use Item action once per rest, you may cast **Read the Earth** at 4th Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Cleansing Slate
Flavor: The dull, featureless gray relieves the disarray of pitched combat when placed at the body's meridians.
Description: As a reaction to being affected by either the poisoned or disoriented condition, you may cleanse yourself of both of those conditions.

Name: Flippermorph Treads
Flavor: A seemingly ordinary pair of boots that, when you click their heels, can transform into some diving flippers.
Description: As a Use Item action, you may enable or disable this item. While enabled and equipped, your Speed is reduced by 5 feet and you gain basic Swimming mobility.

Name: Fractured Agate
Flavor: Insult to injury crystallized within the break lines of its wavy hues.
Description: As a reaction immediately after you make a damage roll, you may choose some of the dice rolled and replace their outcome with their maximum value before the damage is dealt. The number of dice chosen is equal to half your Tier Bonus.

Name: Lucid Spectacles
Flavor: It's not that the distracting details aren't there, it is that you simply see them for what they are.
Description: While equipped, you gain advantage on Search checks.

Name: The S-Cape
Flavor: It stands for Slippery.
Description: While equipped, you gain advantage on Escape checks.

Name: Warding Quartz
Flavor: The angles of the cut are perfectly positioned to absorb and deflect.
Description:
Multiple variants of **Warding Quartz** exist for the following damage types: **acid**, **cold**, **fire**, **force**, **lightning**, **necrotic**, **poison**, **psychic**, and **radiant**.

As a reaction when you are dealt damage of this variant's type, you may gain a DB against that damage type equal to 5 - half your Tier Bonus until the end of the current turn.

Name: Apacknid
Flavor: A handy backpack with retractable spider legs able to carry your stuff and just strong enough to lift you with it.
Description:
As a Use Item action, you may either **Deploy** this item or, once per long rest, **Climb** with it:

* **Deploy.** The item is *deployed* onto a target unoccupied space within 10 feet of you, leaping off of your back with all objects stowed in it and gaining advanced Climbing mobility. Once during each of your turns you may direct the deployed item to travel up to 30 feet. A Pick Up action on this item causes it to no longer be deployed.

* **Climb.** You cause the item to grant its wearer advanced Climbing mobility for 10 minutes.

Name: Carnelian Rose
Flavor: A bold statement, and fortune favors the bold.
Description:
Multiple variants of **Carnelian Rose** exist for the following ability modifiers: **INT**, **WIS**, and **CHA**.

	Once per rest, as a reaction when making an attack check that does not use this variant's ability modifier, you may add this variant's ability modifier to the total.

Name: Championship Belt
Flavor: A weighted belt with a massive golden emblem placed at the center. Each victory claimed by the owner etches a tally mark in the medallion.
Description: While equipped, you gain advantage on Grapple and Pin checks.

Name: Hopeful Opal
Flavor: Endless possibilities twinkle within the scintillating stone.
Description:
Multiple variants of **Hopeful Opal** exist for the following saving throw types: **Poise**, **Reflex**, **Fortitude**, **Quick-Wits**, **Will**, and **Courage**.

As a reaction when you make a saving throw of this variant's type, you may add 1d4 to the total.

Name: Intrusive Chime
Flavor: The pleasant ringing serves as an immediate distraction from whatever blunder you just blurted out.
Description: Once per rest, you may cast **Never Mind** at 1st Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Phantom Strides
Flavor: A gloomy mist swirls around the soles. You tread with no more than a whisper of a footfall.
Description: While equipped, you gain advantage on Vanish checks.

Name: Clouded Diamond
Flavor: Originally mistaken for a hailstone, this gem now turns you into one.
Description: Once per rest, you may cast **Double Jump** at 2nd Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Geminid Gem
Flavor: A pair of rings with a penchant for trading their wearers.
Description:
This item is paired with another **Geminid Gem**. Purchasing or crafting this item creates a copy that is paired to this one.

	Once per long rest, either of the wearers may take a reaction at any point to cast **Translocate** at 2nd Circle using their INT, WIS, or CHA as their spellcasting modifier. The caster must target the other wearer.

Name: Rocket Boots
Flavor: The wings attached on the sides are really just fins. It propels more than it flies.
Description: As a Use Item action once per long rest, you may cause the item to grant its wearer basic Hovering mobility for 10 minutes.

Name: Snowglint Sapphire
Flavor: Set within a silver snowflake, this gem glistens with winter energy.
Description: As a reaction to being affected by either the slowed or frozen condition, you may cleanse yourself of both of those conditions and inflict them on a target creature within 15 feet of you until the end of their next turn.

Name: Solemn Timepiece
Flavor: A wristwatch hums with the perpetuity of procession. Not a moment has gone by without it all falling exactly into place. Not a word need be shared as everything and everyone takes their cue.
Description: While equipped, you gain advantage on Initiate checks.

Name: Cloudwing Cloak
Flavor: A cloak woven from vaporous threading. Light as a feather, you soar through the sky.
Description: While equipped, you gain basic Flying mobility.

Name: Schism Pearl
Flavor: The fissure encased within blinks like a cat's eye, seeing through every ruse with piercing insight.
Description: As a reaction when making an attack check, you may reduce the target's AC to their Base AC for that attack check.

Name: Spellbreaker Bead
Flavor: A finely polished blood stone compressed by the weight of pure magic.
Description: As a reaction, you may cast **Counterspell** at 2nd Circle using your CON as your spellcasting modifier. When you cast **Counterspell** using this item, you deal **5 injuring force damage** to yourself. This damage roll is cumulatively doubled after each use of this item, but it is reset to 5 at the end of your next long rest.

Name: Spirited Peridot
Flavor: Glittering with the spark of life, the moss suspended in the verdant stone seeps with eternal vigor.
Description:
As a reaction when you are about to burn a Resolve, you may instead use this item to gain the effects of burning a Resolve. This item cannot be used again until you succeed a DC 15 flat check, which you may attempt once at the end of each long rest.

# **List of Battlesmith Items**

Name: Firebreath Weapon
Flavor: Its metal is alloyed with gold stolen from a dragon's hoard. Its fury rests without slumber.
Description:
This item can be purposed for any fighting style. This weapon has the following trait and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the trait with a minor reforge trait of your choice.

* **Energized (Pyral).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **fire damage**.

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause all creatures and objects within a 10 foot cone in front of you to make a basic **Reflex** saving throw to resist **fire damage**.

The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **2d8 fire damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **4d8 fire damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **6d8 fire damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **8d8 fire damage.**

Name: Folding Armor
Flavor: A finely-tuned system of interlocking plates that swiftly collapses into a compact form. Despite this intricacy, it is anything but delicate.
Description:
This armor may be any type of armor. This armor has the following trait and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the trait with a minor reforge trait of your choice.

* **Shock-Absorbing.** When you are dealt falling damage, you may halve the damage taken.

	As a Use Item action, you may immediately take off or wear this armor. If you take off this armor and are holding a different armor with the **Quick Collapse** aspect, you may wear that armor as part of the same Use Item action.

Name: Hoarfrost Shield
Flavor: Clawing icicles are emblazoned upon the shield, projecting outward an eternal chill. You form the boundary between where life ends and where winter begins.
Description:
This shield has the following trait and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the trait with a minor reforge trait of your choice.

* **Spiked.** In addition to being a shield, this item also counts as a weapon suitable for throwing, usable for any fighting style that does not have the **Exclusive** trait. You may apply weapon reforge traits and weapon aspects to this item.

	Once per long rest, after taking the Raise Shield action using this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause all spaces within 15 feet of you to act as difficult terrain until the start of your next turn.

Name: Mutate Plate
Flavor: If you know the enemy and know yourself, you need not fear the result of a hundred battles.
Description:
This armor may be any type of armor. This armor has the following trait and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the trait with a minor reforge trait of your choice.

* **Triumphant.** You do not fall prone from burning a Resolve.

	As a Use Item action, you may gain a DB against a **physical damage** type of your choosing until the start of your next turn or you use **Ironweave** again. The DB's amount depends on this item's rarity (5 for Common, 10 for Uncommon, 15 for Rare, and 20 for Legendary).

Name: Windfury Blade
Flavor: Carved into it is the storied legacy of stormclouds. With every strike it shall not be forgotten.
Description:
This item can be purposed for any fighting style. This weapon has the following trait and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the trait with a minor reforge trait of your choice.

* **Returning.** You may perform a one minute ritual to *bind* this item to you. This item may be bound to only one creature. When you take the Retrieve object interaction, you may instead teleport a bound item to you and then hold it.

	Once per long rest, as a Use Item action, you may travel up to your Speed and then make a light weapon attack with this item.

Name: Author's Plume
Flavor: A pinion of the pen that wrote upon the page of the world.
Description:
This item can be purposed for any fighting style. This weapon has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with minor reforge traits of your choice.

* **Attuned.** In addition to being a weapon, this item also counts as a spellcasting focus. You may apply spellcasting focus aspects to this item.

* **Energized (Ethereal).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **force damage**.

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item, you may take a Use Item action to teleport yourself and this item to a target unoccupied space within 30 feet of you. If the weapon attack hit, you may also cause the target of the attack to make a **Poise** saving throw. On a failure, the target is teleported with you.

	The saving throw DC depends on the item's rarity (14 for Common, 17 for Uncommon, 20 for Rare, and 23 for Legendary).

Name: Forceglass Bulwark
Flavor: A panel of arcane energies condensed into a wavy glass. A special conduit resides within the handle to regulate the size and shape of the projected solid.
Description:
This shield has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with two minor reforge traits of your choice.

* **Steady (Tower).** While you gain the benefits of the Raise Shield action or Reactive Block reaction using this item, you gain advantage on saving throws made to resist falling prone.
* **Thunderous.** When you take the Reactive Block reaction using this item against a melee attack and it misses, the attacker incurs disadvantage on the next attack they make this turn.

	As a Use Item action once per long rest, you may drop this item to *project* it and form a 25 foot long, 15 foot high rectangular wall originating in front of you that persists while this item is projected. The wall has a Toughness of 20 and HP based on this item's rarity (10 for Common, 20 for Uncommon, 30 for Rare, and 40 for Legendary). If the wall is destroyed, the effect ends and the shield returns to its original form, dropping onto the ground.

While projected, this item cannot be used as a shield and, if the wall is within your Reach, you may take a Use Item action to end the effect early and hold the shield once more. Only the creature that projected this item may end the effect early in this manner.

Name: Honeysuckle Hauberk
Flavor: A gnarled mass of symbiotic foliage formed into a piece of living armor. As a substitute for chain mail, movement can be a little stiff but it helps the most where it counts.
Description:
This armor may be any type of armor. This armor has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with two minor reforge traits of your choice.

* **Resistant (Curative).** You gain a DB against **poison damage** based on the rarity of the item: 5, 10, 15, and 20 for Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary respectively.

* **Mobile (Stabilized).** You gain basic Climbing mobility.

	As a Use Item action once per long rest, you may cleanse yourself of the poisoned condition and **regain ST**. The ST regained depends on the item's rarity (4d6 for Common, 8d6 for Uncommon, 12d6 for Rare, and 16d6 for Legendary).

Name: Lightning Chakram
Flavor: A coil of scintillating wire bound with magnetic spines, eager to encircle and electrify.
Description:
This item can be purposed for any fighting style and is suitable for throwing. This weapon has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with minor reforge traits of your choice.

* **Energized (Electric).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **lightning damage**.

* **Returning.** You may perform a one minute ritual to *bind* this item to you. This item may be bound to only one creature. When you take the Retrieve object interaction, you may instead teleport a bound item to you and then hold it.

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item against a target within 10 feet of you, you may take a Use Item action to cause all creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you to make a basic **Poise** saving throw to resist **lightning damage**. On a failure, an affected creature is also pulled 5 feet closer to you to an unoccupied space.

The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **2d8 lightning damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **4d8 lightning damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **6d8 lightning damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **8d8 lightning damage.**

Name: Sea Serpent Cuirass
Flavor: The shifting hues of the scales form an animate image of a stormy sea. As you call upon its magic, lightning crackles in the backdrop.
Description:
This armor may be any type of armor. This armor has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with two minor reforge traits of your choice.

* **Finned.** You gain basic Swimming mobility.

* **Resistant (Shimmering).** You gain a DB against **radiant damage** based on the rarity of the item: 5, 10, 15, and 20 for Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary respectively.

	As a Use Item action once per long rest, you may cause all creatures of your choosing within 10 feet of you to make a **Poise** saving throw. The DC of the saving throw depends on the rarity of the item (14 for Common, 17 for Uncommon, 20 for Rare, and 23 for Legendary). On a failure, an affected creature is pushed 10 feet away from you.

Name: Bullseye Buckler
Flavor: It's got big red circles painted on it - of course they're going to aim for it.
Description:
This shield has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with reforge traits of your choice of at most one major reforge trait.

* **Steady (Bold).** While you gain the benefits of the Raise Shield action or Reactive Block reaction using this item, you gain advantage on saving throws made to resist being frightened.
* **Thunderous.** When you take the Reactive Block reaction using this item against a melee attack and it misses, the attacker incurs disadvantage on the next attack they make this turn.
* **Stalwart (Elusive).** While you gain the benefits of the Raise Shield action or Reactive Block reaction using this item, you gain advantage on **Reflex** saving throws.

	As a reaction once per long rest, if you are within 15 of a ranged attack's targeting path, you may cause the attack to target you instead and gain the benefits of the Reactive Block reaction against the attack.

Name: Forgebound Plating
Flavor: A suit of armor drawn from tempered slag forged in magma. Trapped within is a volcanic might few can claim to best.
Description:
This armor is Fortified armor. This armor has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with reforge traits of your choice of at most one major reforge trait.

* **Resistant (Hearthwrought).** You gain a DB against **fire damage** based on the rarity of the item: 5, 10, 15, and 20 for Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary respectively.

* **Triumphant.** You do not fall prone from burning a Resolve.

* **Anchored.** You cannot be pushed or pulled.

	As a Use Item action once per long rest, you may gain the following benefits for 10 minutes or until your ST breaks or you expend a Resolve:

* **Enwreathed.** If this armor has the **Resistant** reforge trait, its DB is doubled and creatures and objects other than you within 5 feet of you for the first time since your most recent turn are dealt damage of the same type as resisted by the **Resistant** reforge trait. The damage dealt depends on the item's rarity (5 for Common, 10 for Uncommon, 15 for Rare, and 20 for Legendary).

* **Welded.** Creatures you have grappled incur disadvantage on Escape checks.

Name: Skyrunner Wraps
Flavor: Tough bandages that, while firm, keep a loose grip on your skin. Shrill winds circle around as you leap through the air.
Description:
This armor is Agile armor. This armor has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with reforge traits of your choice of at most one major reforge trait.

* **Mobile (Springed).** You gain basic Jumping mobility.

* **Shock-Absorbing.** When you are dealt falling damage, you may halve the damage taken.

* **Swift.** Your Speed is increased by 10 feet.

	As a Use Item action once per long rest, you may gain advanced Hovering mobility then travel up to your Speed. This Hovering mobility persists until either the end of your next turn or you move again, and all ranged attacks against you automatically miss while you have this Hovering mobility.

Name: Truthbearer
Flavor: A trusted battering ram against which no fortress of deceit may hold. Even amidst pitch darkness it carries the glint of the morning sun.
Description:
This item can be purposed for any fighting style. This weapon has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with minor reforge traits and up to one major reforge trait of your choice.

* **Energized (Sunlit).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **radiant damage**.

* **Returning.** You may perform a one minute ritual to *bind* this item to you. This item may be bound to only one creature. When you take the Retrieve object interaction, you may instead teleport a bound item to you and then hold it.

* **Keen.** The critical hit threshold of weapon attacks made with this item is reduced by 1\.

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause the target of the attack to make a basic **Reflex** saving throw to resist **radiant damage**. On a failure, an affected subject may not gain the benefits of the invisible condition and all illusions they create or have created become obvious for the next 10 minutes.

The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **2d12 radiant damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **4d12 radiant damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **6d12 radiant damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **8d12 radiant damage.**

Name: Winterwing Bow
Flavor: A bow blessed by a boreal phoenix in both its grace and ferocity.
Description:
This weapon is purposed for either the Basic Ranged or Loading Ranged fighting styles. This weapon has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with minor reforge traits and up to one major reforge trait of your choice.

* **Attuned.** In addition to being a weapon, this item also counts as a spellcasting focus. You may apply spellcasting focus aspects to this item.

* **Energized (Frigid).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **cold damage**.

* **Seeking.** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, the target's AC is reduced by one guard step until the end of the current turn (they may either have their Full Guard AC, Half Guard AC, or Base AC).

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause the target of the attack and all creatures and objects within 10 feet of it to make a basic **Reflex** saving throw to resist **cold damage**. On a failure, an affected creature is slowed until the end of their next turn.

The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **2d8 cold damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **4d8 cold damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **6d8 cold damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **8d8 cold damage.**

Name: Abyssal Veil
Flavor: A dreadful set of armor that weighs heavier with each defiance of the inevitable. As ghastly demise concentrates in the palm of your hand, you know it is never without cost.
Description:
This armor may be any type of armor. This armor has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with reforge traits of your choice of at most two major reforge traits.

* **Resistant (Soulful).** You gain a DB against **necrotic** damage based on the rarity of the item: 5, 10, 15, and 20 for Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary respectively.

* **Triumphant.** You do not fall prone from burning a Resolve.

* **Adamantine.** Critical hits scored against you instead count as normal hits.

* **Anchored.** You cannot be pushed or pulled.

	When you burn a Resolve while equipped, this item gains one *abyssal charge* that remains until used or until you take a Refresh downtime activity. As a Use Item action, you may expend an abyssal charge from the item to cause a target creature within 60 feet of you to make a basic **Will** saving throw to resist **necrotic damage**. On a failure, an affected creature is incapacitated until the end of its next turn. The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **3d8 necrotic damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **6d8 necrotic damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **9d8 necrotic damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **12d8 necrotic damage.**

Name: Calcified Dread
Flavor: A lingering thought made manifest as a spindling dagger burying deeper and deeper into the nightmares of your foes.
Description:
This weapon can be purposed for any fighting style. This weapon has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with minor reforge traits and up to two major reforge traits of your choice.

* **Disguised.** You may determine a *disguised form* for the item, which may be any ordinary object of roughly the same Size and shape. For example, you could designate the disguised form of a sword to be a cane. While equipped, you may take an object interaction to cause this item to either transform into its disguised form or return it to its original form.

* **Energized (Illusory).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **psychic damage**.

* **Seeking.** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, the target's AC is reduced by one guard step until the end of the current turn (they may either have their Full Guard AC, Half Guard AC, or Base AC).

* **Spectral.** When you make weapon attacks using this item, the targeting path does not need to be unobstructed.

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause the target of the attack to make a **Quick-Wits** saving throw. On a failure, the target is *distressed* for 10 minutes. When a creature distressed by this aspect targets you, your occupied space, or your location, they are dealt **psychic damage** and afterwards becomes disoriented until the end of the current turn.

The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **2d6 psychic damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **4d6 psychic damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **6d6 psychic damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **8d6 psychic damage.**

Name: Dream Ward
Flavor: An object conceived at the boundary between rest and dream, where the skewing of logic produces a barrier against all manner of attack and possibility.
Description:
This shield has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with reforge traits of your choice of at most one major reforge trait.

* **Spiked.** In addition to being a shield, this item also counts as a weapon suitable for throwing, usable for any fighting style that does not have the **Exclusive** trait. You may apply weapon reforge traits and weapon aspects to this item.
* **Steady (Resolute).** While you gain the benefits of the Raise Shield action or Reactive Block reaction using this item, you gain advantage on saving throws made to resist being disoriented.
* **Cloaking.** When you take the Raise Shield action using this item, you become invisible until you are either hit with an attack or until you no longer benefit from the Raise Shield action.
* **Solid.** While you gain the benefits of the Raise Shield action or Reactive Block reaction using this item, you gain advantage on saving throws made to resist becoming incapacitated, stunned, or frozen.

	Once per long rest, after taking the Raise Shield action using this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause all creatures in a 10 foot cone originating in front of you to make a **Quick-Wits** saving throw. The DC of the saving throw depends on the rarity of this item (14 for Common, 17 for Uncommon, 20 for Rare, and 23 for Legendary). On a failure, a creature becomes unconscious without its **Prone** condition trait (they do not fall prone).

Name: Ichorforged Maul
Flavor: A foul monument of blasphemy dredged from the depths of the planarverse.
Description:
This weapon is purposed for the Heavy fighting style. This weapon has the following traits and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with minor reforge traits and up to two major reforge traits of your choice.

* **Energized (Venomous).** When you hit with a weapon attack using this item, you may convert all of the attack's damage to **poison damage**.

* **Specialized (Weighted).** Once per rest while equipped, you may negate the repetition penalty incurred when you perform **Wallop**.

* **Keen.** The critical hit threshold of weapon attacks made with this item is reduced by 1\.

* **Obliterating.** While equipped, you gain advantage on Wreck an Object checks and attacks against objects using this item deal double damage.

	Once per long rest, after you make a weapon attack with this item, you may take a Use Item action to cause the target of the attack to make a **Fortitude** saving throw to resist **poison damage**. On a failure, the target is poisoned for 10 minutes. A creature poisoned by this aspect may retry the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. The saving throw's DC and the damage dealt is based on the item's rarity:

* **Common**. DC 14 against **2d10 poison damage**.

* **Uncommon**. DC 17 against **4d10 poison damage**.

* **Rare**. DC 20 against **6d10 poison damage.**

* **Legendary**. DC 23 against **8d10 poison damage.**

Name: Stellar Mantle
Flavor: A well-worn coat embroidered with starlight and fashioned with astral compasses. As nebulae and liquid worlds roil through the endless space of spaces, only a true north finds its way through.
Description:
This armor may be any type of armor. This armor has the following trait and aspect. When you purchase or craft this item, you may replace the traits with reforge traits of your choice of at most two major reforge traits.

	asdfas

* **Resistant (Planelinked).** You gain a DB against **force** damage based on the rarity of the item: 5, 10, 15, and 20 for Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary respectively.

* **Shock-Absorbing.** When you are dealt falling damage, you may halve the damage taken.

* **Adamantine.** Critical hits scored against you instead count as normal hits.

* **Swift.** Your Speed is increased by 10 feet.

	As a Use Item action, you may either **Point** or, once per long rest, **Guide**:

* **Point.** You make a ranged special attack using your INT, WIS, or CHA against a target within 60 feet of you. On a hit, a target is *aligned* to you for 10 minutes and dealt **radiant damage** based on the item's rarity (2d6 for Common, 4d6 for Uncommon, 6d6 for Rare, and 8d6 for Legendary). A target of this attack may allow it to automatically hit and deal no damage.

* **Guide.** You teleport all creatures and objects aligned by **Point** to unoccupied spaces within your Reach.

Name: Armor Reforge (Uncommon)
Flavor: Arduous effort is hammered into the plating, emboldening the magic within it.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Uncommon. You may add a minor armor reforge trait to it.

Name: Shield Reforge (Uncommon)
Flavor: Polished to an immaculate shine.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Uncommon. You may add a minor shield reforge trait to it.

Name: Weapon Reforge (Uncommon)
Flavor: Folded, bright steel is honed to deadly precision.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Uncommon. You may add a minor weapon reforge trait to it.

Name: Armor Reforge (Rare)
Flavor: Everything from the fittings to the protective pieces are perfectly suited for its wearer.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Rare. You may add a minor or major armor reforge trait to it.

Name: Shield Reforge (Rare)
Flavor: An emblem of a cause forged in the fires of passion.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Rare. You may add a minor or major shield reforge trait to it.

Name: Weapon Reforge (Rare)
Flavor: Hardest adamant with a razor edge.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Rare. You may add a minor or major weapon reforge trait to it.

Name: Armor Reforge (Legendary)
Flavor: Tested through time and battle, the craftsmanship proves its own legacy.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Legendary. You may add a minor or major armor reforge trait to it.

Name: Shield Reforge (Legendary)
Flavor: An emblazoned crest is affixed, now inextricably linked with your name and legend.
Description: The item's rarity is upgraded to Legendary. You may add a minor or major shield reforge trait to it.

Name: Weapon Reforge (Legendary)
Flavor: A name is etched into it, forged in battle and quenched in true purpose.
Description:
The item's rarity is upgraded to Legendary. You may add a minor or major weapon reforge trait to it.

*Minor Armor Reforge Trait*

	Multiple variants of **Mobile** exist as listed below. You gain a basic mobility as determined by one of the following variants:

* **Finned.** You gain basic Swimming mobility.

* **Springed.** You gain basic Jumping mobility.

* **Stabilized.** You gain basic Climbing mobility.

*Minor Armor Reforge Trait*

	Multiple variants of **Resistant** exist as listed below. You gain a DB based on the rarity of the item: 5, 10, 15, and 20 for Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary respectively. The type of damage resisted by the DB is determined by one of the following variants:

* **Neutralized.** The DB is against **acid damage**.

* **Rimeweft.** The DB is against **cold damage**.

* **Hearthwrought.** The DB is against **fire damage**.

* **Planelinked.** The DB is against **force damage**.

* **Grounded.** The DB is against **lightning damage**.

* **Soulful.** The DB is against **necrotic damage**.

* **Curative.** The DB is against **poison damage**.

* **Judicious.** The DB is against **psychic damage**.

* **Shimmering.** The DB is against **radiant damage**.

*Minor Armor Reforge Trait*

	When you are dealt falling damage, you may halve the damage taken.

*Minor Armor Reforge Trait*

	You do not fall prone from burning a Resolve.

*Major Armor Reforge Trait*

	Critical hits scored against you instead count as normal hits.

*Major Armor Reforge Trait*

	You cannot be pushed or pulled.

*Major Armor Reforge Trait*

	While worn, your Speed is increased by 10 feet.

Name: Spell Scroll
Flavor: I can be anything, take a look.
Description:
This item can exist at multiple rarities. The Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary variants of this item have a crafting threshold score of 30/40/50/60 respectively, and other statistics shown below use this same notation.

	A spell of baseline Circle 1st/2nd/3rd/4th is *written* onto this item (when created from a stock recipe, the spell is chosen when the stock recipe's crafting project begins rather than when this item is produced). The written spell has one of the following limitations:

* **If you craft this item**, the spell must be from one of the spell lists chosen for your Scrivener specialization.

* **If you purchase this item**, the GM determines the available spell list(s) from which you are able to choose.

As a Use Item action, you may expend this item to cast the spell written on this item at 1st/2nd/3rd/4th Circle using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.

Name: Embalmed Eye
Flavor: A perfectly preserved specimen complete with a frozen reflection of its last moment alive.
Description:
Once per long rest, you may recall a moment with perfect clarity that you had experienced an hour or more earlier but since the end of your last long rest. Until you dismiss this effect as an action, your senses are replaced with what you had at that moment. While in this state, you gain advantage on all INT and WIS effort and trade checks examining details in the recalled moment.

This item is imbued with a certain number of *Spell Charges* depending on its rarity (1 for Common, 2 for Uncommon, 3 for Rare, and 4 for Legendary). As a Use Item action, you may cast and heighten the spells from the list below by expending a number of Spell Charges equal to the Circle of spell cast. You may use your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier when casting these spells:

* 1st Circle: **Life Ball**

When you complete a long rest, all expended Spell Charges imbued into the item are restored.

Name: Mourning Crest
Flavor: A relic shedding tears of dew with each dawn of a new day. Those left behind remain closer still.
Description:
Once per rest, as a Use Item action, you may learn if there are any undead creatures within 60 feet of you.

	This item is imbued with a certain number of *Spell Charges* depending on its rarity (1 for Common, 2 for Uncommon, 3 for Rare, and 4 for Legendary). As a Use Item action, you may cast and heighten the spells from the list below by expending a number of Spell Charges equal to the Circle of spell cast. You may use your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier when casting these spells:

* 1st Circle: **Armor of Souls**

When you complete a long rest, all expended Spell Charges imbued into the item are restored.

Name: Stardust Wand
Flavor: Carved from a tree that grew on an asteroid that fell to the earth, so the legend goes.
Description:
You may cast **Sign** and produce its **Light** effect using your INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting modifier.
